headline: Is ConocoPhillips (COP) Still Attractively Priced After Its Strong Recent Share Price Run?
summary: If you are wondering whether ConocoPhillips at around US$129.35 is still attractively priced or already reflecting a lot of optimism, you are asking the right question for a valuation check. The stock has shown strong recent moves, with returns of 5.3% over 7 days, 17.0% over 30 days, 33.8% year to date, 30.5% over 1 year, 45.2% over 3 years and 189.4% over 5 years. These price moves sit against a backdrop of ongoing investor focus on large oil and gas producers. Factors like commodity...

headline: 3 US Integrated Energy Stocks to Gain Despite Industry Headwinds

headline: Halliburton’s $42 Price Target From BMO Signals More Runway After a Monster 2026
summary: Halliburton (NYSE:HAL) has been on a strong run heading into spring 2026. The stock is up 6.24% over the past week, 9.53% over the past month and 30.51% year to date, recently touching a 52-week high of $38.68. Over the past year, shares have climbed 50.37%. headline: ConocoPhillips Seeks US Protection As Qatar Risks Test LNG Growth Story
summary: ConocoPhillips (NYSE:COP) has urged the US government to increase protection for Qatari energy assets after Iranian strikes damaged key facilities. The company has reported operational disruption in Qatar, including the evacuation of some staff and heightened security protocols. Management is framing the situation as a material geopolitical and operational risk to its international portfolio. For investors tracking NYSE:COP, this security request comes as the stock trades around $129.35,...
